name: Leihs
researched_as: Leihs equipment lending and inventory system
license: AGPL-3.0 (verify current repo before bundling code)
what: Open-source equipment booking, inventory, procurement, and lending system designed around loan workflows.
solves: Reservation, lending, returns, inventory visibility, and operational tracking for equipment that moves between users.
users: Organizations managing loaner equipment, libraries, institutions, and structured lending workflows.
fit: Very strong conceptual fit for Hylono rental/access programs. It is materially closer to Hylono’s lending/rental reality than generic IT asset tools.
runtime: Rails-based application stack with database, background jobs, mailers, and operational workflows around reservations and lending.
components: Web app, relational database, workers/jobs, mail, storage, and role-based operational UI.
deploy: The leihs-instance repo is the most useful public deployment blueprint; production still requires standard app hardening, storage, backups, and role design.
deps: Database, mailer, storage, background jobs, reverse proxy/TLS, and operational process configuration.
auth: Application roles and authentication; verify current SSO integration options if required.
apis: Operational web UI and app interfaces; API surface should be verified against current version before integration assumptions.
security: Reservation histories and user assignments may include personal data. Access control and auditability matter for rental operations.
setup_path: Begin with leihs-instance and the main repo docs. Validate whether its workflow model matches Hylono rental logistics before deep implementation.
env: App secrets, DB settings, mailer, storage paths, base URLs, worker settings, and any identity/SMTP integrations.
persistence: Back up database and uploaded files; treat reservations and lending records as operational truth if adopted.
operations: User role mapping, reservation policy tuning, upgrade testing, and reporting/export checks.
failure_points: Underestimating workflow adaptation, ignoring customer-facing UX requirements, and assuming institutional defaults match premium commercial rental operations.
integration: Strong candidate to back rental/access programs, equipment availability, reservation states, and handoff/return operations. Could integrate with Next.js booking flows, CRM, notifications, and billing.
verify: Confirm current API/integration surface, multilingual/customer-facing UX suitability, and whether Hylono wants Leihs as the rental source of truth or only as the back-office engine.
risk: Moderate. Best functional fit for rentals, but likely needs adaptation work and careful UX/process mapping.
gdpr: Contains identifiable booking and equipment assignment data; EU hosting is a positive fit if deployed carefully.
overlap: Preferred over Snipe-IT for lending/rental. Could overlap partly with custom booking flows or Fleetbase logistics later.
codex_read_first: Read the main repo and leihs-instance deployment blueprint before proposing rental-system design.
codex_best_path: Prototype Leihs as a back-office rental engine first. Expose only the customer-facing pieces needed through Hylono’s Next.js site or APIs.
codex_never_assume: Never assume Leihs customer UX is ready for direct exposure without adaptation. Never assume its institutional workflow defaults match commercial rental needs.
